prompt: Pharmaceutical vial product photography, vertical 4:5 portrait composition. The reference image shows the locked vial geometry, navy blue cap (hex #101850), studio lighting, label material, and bottom accent bar — reproduce all of these identically. Cap color must be EXACTLY hex #101850, do not drift lighter or darker, do not shift hue. Vial size: 3ml, exact proportions matching the reference. The label has the following content laid out top to bottom: (1) Upper roughly 60 percent of the label is a blank pristine white logo zone with absolutely no graphics, text, lines, or marks of any kind — generous empty space reserved for a future logo overlay. LOCKED TYPEFACE FOR ALL TEXT: Helvetica Neue (or visually identical clean geometric medical sans-serif). Use this single typeface family for every text element on the label — do not mix typefaces. (2) Below the logo zone, the product name 'MOTS-C' rendered in HELVETICA NEUE BOLD (weight ~700) in dark charcoal grey, perfectly horizontally centered on the label, natural letter proportions (NOT vertically stretched, NOT condensed). (3) Directly below the product name with tight spacing, the dose-and-volume line '10 mg · 3 ml' rendered in HELVETICA NEUE BOLD (the SAME bold weight as the product name) at roughly 60 percent of the product-name size, charcoal grey, perfectly horizontally centered on the label, with a single middle-dot separator. (4) Below the dose with a small visual gap, the compliance text rendered as TWO STACKED LINES, each line perfectly horizontally centered on the label, both lines in HELVETICA NEUE LIGHT (~300 weight, definitely NOT bold), all-caps, in medium grey, at roughly 22 percent of the product-name size, with tight line spacing. Line 1: 'FOR RESEARCH USE ONLY' (ends after 'ONLY' with no trailing punctuation). Line 2: 'NOT FOR HUMAN CONSUMPTION' (starts with 'NOT'). Both lines are equal in size, weight, and color, stacked tightly together as an intentional two-line disclaimer block — NOT a wrap, but a deliberate formatted stack. (5) Directly below the second compliance line with a small gap, the lot line 'LOT: NH-1024' rendered in HELVETICA NEUE LIGHT (the SAME thin/light weight as the compliance lines, NOT bold), at the same small size, in medium grey, on a single line. ABSOLUTE CENTERING REQUIREMENT FOR THE LOT LINE — the lot text MUST be perfectly horizontally centered on the label, with EQUAL margins of empty space to the LEFT and RIGHT of the text. Do NOT left-align the lot. Do NOT right-align it. Do NOT shift it toward either side. The horizontal center of the 'LOT: NH-1024' string must align exactly with the horizontal center of the label. Treat this as a non-negotiable layout rule: every other text element on this label is centered, and the lot line must match that centering exactly. CRITICAL TYPOGRAPHY HIERARCHY — Helvetica Neue Bold for product name + dose; Helvetica Neue Light for compliance + lot. CRITICAL ALIGNMENT — all FIVE text elements (product name, dose line, compliance line 1, compliance line 2, lot line) are horizontally centered on the label with EQUAL left and right margins. None of them are left-aligned, none are right-aligned. The lot line in particular must be center-aligned to match the rest — verify the lot text horizontal center matches the label horizontal center. CRITICAL LINE-COUNT — compliance is exactly two lines (line 1 'FOR RESEARCH USE ONLY', line 2 'NOT FOR HUMAN CONSUMPTION'), each perfectly centered; lot line is exactly one line, also centered. No element wraps unexpectedly. (6) At the very bottom edge of the label, the slim solid accent bar at exactly hex #101850 running edge to edge. Absolutely NO hairlines anywhere on the label, NO horizontal rules above or below the text elements — just the single solid bar at the bottom. All text elements are perfectly horizontally centered and stacked tight in the lower portion of the label below the empty logo zone. CRITICAL: render every text string exactly as written, no abbreviations, no spelling changes. Sharp focus, high resolution, premium pharmaceutical product shot, clinical minimalist aesthetic.
